errors: The ELM implementation does not account for the 20 pack-year smoking history as recommended by the USPSTF, and the age thresholds and lookback periods seem to be correctly implemented but the smoking history condition only checks for active tobacco use without considering the pack-year history.

The age thresholds in the ELM implementation (Age >= 60 years and Age <= 75 years) do not match the typical clinical guidelines for colorectal cancer screening, which usually recommend screening for average-risk individuals starting at age 50 and continuing until at least age 75, with some guidelines suggesting screening up to age 85 or beyond, depending on the individual's health status and life expectancy. The lookback period of 2 years for recent colorectal screening may also not align with all clinical guidelines, as the recommended screening interval can vary based on the screening modality and individual risk factors.

The age threshold in the ELM implementation (30-65 years) does not match the recommended age range in the clinical practice guideline (21-65 years). The ELM implementation requires adjustment to align with the CPG recommendation.

The age threshold in the ELM implementation is 75 years, but the USPSTF recommendation is for adults 65 years or older. This discrepancy may lead to patients between 65-74 years old being missed for falls prevention screening.

The age threshold in the ELM implementation is 25 years, but the USPSTF recommends screening for unhealthy alcohol use in adults 18 years or older. This discrepancy may lead to missed screenings for individuals between 18 and 24 years old.

The age threshold in the ELM implementation (Age >= 55 years and Age <= 75 years) does not match the recommended age range in the Clinical Practice Guideline (CPG) which is men aged 65-75 years who have ever smoked. The ELM implementation includes men aged 55-64 years who may not be recommended for screening according to the CPG.

The ELM implementation does not fully align with the clinical practice guidelines (CPG). Specifically, the CPG recommends annual LDCT for lung cancer screening in adults aged 50-80 years with a 20 pack-year smoking history, but the ELM implementation only checks for age >= 50 years and does not account for the 20 pack-year smoking history or the upper age limit of 80 years. Additionally, the ELM implementation does not specify the pack-year smoking history in the "Has Smoking History" logic definition, which may lead to inaccurate identification of patients who meet the smoking history criteria.
